results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need for ...
results.content:
We present EurekAgent, an agent system for autonomous scientific discovery that coordinates off-the-shelf CLI agents through four environment engineering dimensions: (1) permissions engineering, to expose useful capabilities and resources while preventing research-integrity violations; (2) artifact engineering, to structure solutions, logs, and evaluation results as shared progress memory; (3) budget engineering, to enable budget-aware exploration with runtime and compute boundaries; and (4) human-in-the-loop engineering, to support easy human supervision and intervention.
Within this environment, the agent remains free to select its own research workflows and strategies. [...] We presented EurekAgent, an environment-engineered system for autonomous scientific discovery on metric-driven research tasks. Rather than prescribing detailed research
workflows, EurekAgent coordinates off-the-shelf CLI-agent sessions through a simple prepare-propose-implement loop, while shaping the surrounding environment for reliable
evaluation, shared progress memory, resource boundaries, and human oversight.
Using Claude Code as the CLI agent and GLM-5.1 as the base LLM, EurekAgent achieves new state-of-the-art results on all evaluated mathematics and kernel engineering tasks, and
ranks first on our evaluated MLE-Bench Lite subset. These results suggest that, as general-purpose CLI agents become more capable, carefully engineered scientific-discovery [...] We frame this
as environment engineering: building environments that amplify productive behaviors, such as open-ended exploration, systematic artifact management, and inter-agent collaboration, while suppressing harmful behaviors, such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight.
We present EurekAgent, an environment-engineered agent system for
metric-driven autonomous scientific discovery.
EurekAgent engineers the environment along four dimensions: permissions engineering for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; artifact engineering for filesystem and Git-based collaboration; budget engineering for budget-aware exploration; and human-in-the-loop engineering for easy human supervision and intervention.
EurekAgent sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple

results.content: suppressing harmful behaviors, such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight. We present EurekAgent, an environment-engineered agent system for metric-driven autonomous scientific discovery. EurekAgent engineers the environment along four dimensions: permissions engineering for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; artifact engineering for filesystem and Git-based collaboration; budget engineering for budget-aware exploration; and human-in-the-loop engineering for easy human supervision and intervention. EurekAgent sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple mathematics, kernel engineering, and machine learning tasks, including new state-of-the-art 26-circle packing results discovered with less than $11 in total API cost. We open-source our code and results, and [...] As model capabilities continue to improve, we argue that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from prescribing agent workflows to designing agent environments: the resources, constraints, and interfaces that shape agent behavior. We frame this as environment engineering: building environments that amplify productive behaviors, such as open-ended exploration, systematic artifact management, and inter-agent collaboration, while suppressing harmful behaviors, such as reward hacking and high-friction human oversight. We present EurekAgent, an environment-engineered agent system for metric-driven autonomous scientific discovery. EurekAgent engineers the environment along four dimensions: permissions engineering for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; [...] for bounded agent execution and isolated evaluation; artifact engineering for filesystem and Git-based collaboration; budget engineering for budget-aware exploration; and human-in-the-loop engineering for easy human supervision and intervention. EurekAgent sets new state-of-the-art results on multiple mathematics, kernel engineering, and machine learning tasks, including new state-of-the-art 26-circle packing results discovered with less than $11 in total API cost. We open-source our code and results, and call for environment engineering as a core research direction for developing reliable autonomous research agents.

Arxiv   PDF   Mendeley [...] LLM-based agents have shown increasing potential in automating scientific discovery. Given an optimizable metric and an execution environment, they can propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, and have produced results that outperform human-designed approaches. As model capabilities continue to improve, we argue that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from prescribing agent workflows to designing agent environments: the resources, constraints, and interfaces that shape agent behavior. results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
results.content:
2



Preprint

We present E UREK A GENT, an agent system for autonomous scientific discovery that coordinates

off-the-shelf CLI agents through four environment engineering dimensions: (1) permissions engi-

neering, to expose useful capabilities and resources while preventing research-integrity violations;

(2) artifact engineering, to structure solutions, logs, and evaluation results as shared progress mem-

ory; (3) budget engineering, to enable budget-aware exploration with runtime and compute bound-

aries; and (4) human-in-the-loop engineering, to support easy human supervision and intervention.

Within this environment, the agent remains free to select its own research workflows and strategies. [...] We evaluate E UREK A GENT on metric-driven research tasks spanning mathematics, kernel engi-

neering, and machine learning engineering. Using off-the-shelf CLI agents and environment-level

design, E UREK A GENT achieves new state-of-the-art results across all mathematics and kernel en-

gineering tasks, and ranks first on the evaluated MLE-Bench subset.Furthermore, with Claude

Code as the CLI agent and GLM-5.1 as the base model, E UREK A GENT achieves new state-of-the-

art results on the three mathematics tasks with an average API cost below $17, where the 26-circle

packing task achieves the lowest API cost of $11. We call for environment engineering as a core

research direction for building capable, efficient, and responsible autonomous research agents.

2 R ELATED W ORK [...] face (Nam et al., 2026; Qu et al., 2026). At the same time, analyses of real reward-hacking incidents

show that agents can exploit weak evaluation protocols, contaminate evidence, or violate procedural

assumptions (Luo et al., 2025; Kokoromyti, 2026; Anthropic, 2026). Instruction-following failures

in complex agentic settings further suggest that reliability cannot be delegated entirely to prompt

engineering (Qi et al., 2025). Some existing work therefore explores environment design to avoid

common failures, but these are usually introduced as task-specific safeguards. E UREK A GENT makes

environment engineering the central design objective: it organizes permissions, artifacts, budgets,

and human oversight as first-class mechanisms for supporting open-ended agent exploration while

results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery (Jun 2
results.content:
Summary:
This paper introduces EUREKAGENT, an agent system that shifts the focus of autonomous scientific discovery from prescribing agent workflows to 'environment engineering.' By structuring permissions, artifacts, budgets, and human-in-the-loop interfaces, the system enables LLM-based agents to autonomously propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions. EUREKAGENT achieved new state-of-the-art results in mathematics, kernel engineering (TriMul), and machine learning (MLE-Bench) tasks.

Key Topics:
- Autonomous Scientific Discovery
- Environment Engineering
- Large Language Model Agents
- Mathematical Optimization
- Kernel Engineering
- Machine Learning Competitions [...] Welcome to AI Paper Slop. So today, we're jumping right into a brand-new paper called Eureka Agent. Agent Environment Engineering is all you need for autonomous scientific discovery. It's by Amy Xin and a team of researchers from Tsinghua university and Renmin university. It is a fascinating read.. It really is, and we're going to get straight to the core insight because this one is urgent. Basically, we have hit a huge bottleneck in AI scientific discovery. current systems, they try to hard-code these rigid step-by-step workflows for AI to follow. Right. And Eureka Agent proves that is just the completely wrong approach, capable general-purpose agents like Claude Code or whatever you're using. They don't need to be micromanaged. What they need is environment engineering. Yes, exactly. So [...] circle packing sounds simple, but it is a notorious nightmare to optimize. The objective is to maximize the radii of 26 non-overlapping circles inside a unit square. Because it's this high-dimensional non-convex space, the moment you adjust the radius or position of one circle. It cascades. Exactly. It cascades and forces you to recalculate the optimal positions of all the others. There are a massive number of local minima that algorithms just get trapped in. Yeah, it requires both broad exploration and incredibly precise local optimization. Right. So Eureka Agent was unleashed on this and it hit a brand new state-of-the-art score of 2.635999.. Wow. But the score itself isn't even the most impressive part. It is how the agent utilized the environment to achieve it. Walk us through the

results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery | alphaXiv
results.content:
John: To wrap up, EUREKAGENT provides strong evidence for a paradigm shift. As large language models become more powerful general problem-solvers, the critical challenge for autonomous science may not be inventing ever-more-complex agent architectures. Instead, it's about designing the operational context that channels their capabilities toward reliable, reproducible, and trustworthy discovery. The key takeaway is this: it's not about building a smarter scientist; it's about building a better lab for the scientist you already have.

John: Thanks for listening. If you have any further questions, ask our AI assistant or drop a comment. [...] John: It's about creating what the paper, referencing Gibson's theory, calls 'affordances'. The environment is designed to make the 'right' actions easy and the 'wrong' actions impossible. Instead of telling the agent how to do research, you create a world where good research practices are the only viable path. EUREKAGENT's main contribution is demonstrating this philosophy through a system that coordinates off-the-shelf agents. It aims to achieve state-of-the-art results not by creating a new agent architecture, but by meticulously crafting the resources, constraints, and interfaces within which an existing agent operates. This shifts the research focus from agent logic to environmental design.

Noah: So what does this 'engineered environment' actually look like in practice? [...] Transcript

John: Welcome to Advanced AI Systems. Today's lecture is on 'EUREKAGENT: AGENT ENVIRONMENT ENGINEERING IS ALL YOU NEED FOR AUTONOMOUS SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERY'. We've seen a lot of recent work like 'ShinkaEvolve' and 'AlphaResearch' focusing on complex evolutionary systems or prescribed workflows for AI agents. This paper, from researchers at Tsinghua University, challenges that trend. It proposes that as models become more capable, the primary focus should shift from designing the agent itself to engineering the environment it operates in.

John: Yes, Noah?

Noah: Excuse me, Professor. So, to put it simply, instead of trying to build a smarter AI scientist from scratch, they're focused on building a better, safer laboratory for a general-purpose AI to work in?

results.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
results.content:
EUREKAGENT addresses this shift by introducing the concept of Environment Engineering. Rather than meticulously prescribing how an agent should think or research, this approach focuses on crafting the resources, constraints, and interfaces of the agent's operational context. By shaping the environment to prevent harmful behaviors (like evaluation tampering) and promote productive ones (like systematic artifact management), EUREKAGENT enables off-the-shelf agents to achieve state-of-the-art results in mathematics, kernel engineering, and machine learning.

Image 1: EUREKAGENT System Architecture_Figure 1: The EUREKAGENT architecture, illustrating the iterative research loop and the four dimensions of environment engineering._

## From Prescribed Workflows to Environment Affordances [...] ## Conclusion

EUREKAGENT demonstrates that the focus of autonomous scientific discovery research should shift toward Environment Engineering. By providing agents with a structured, secure, and resource-managed environment, it is possible to transform general-purpose LLMs into highly effective researchers. The system's ability to achieve state-of-the-art results across mathematics and engineering—often with minimal cost—suggests a scalable path forward for AI-driven science. By open-sourcing the system, the authors provide a framework for the community to build more robust and reliable autonomous research agents.

AlphaEvolve: A coding agent for scientific and algorithmic discovery [...] This benchmark paper provides the core motivation for EUREKAGENT's design philosophy. Its finding that general-purpose agents can outperform specialized research systems supports the main argument that the bottleneck for autonomous discovery is shifting from designing agent workflows to engineering agent environments.
